title: Minimize the admission of root containers

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of root containers
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run as the root user.
input_row.rationale: Containers may run as any Linux user. Containers which run as the root user, whilst constrained by Container Runtime security features still have an escalated likelihood of container breakout. Ideally, all containers should run as a defined non-UID 0 user. There should be at least one PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) defined which does not permit root users in a container. If you need to run root containers, this should be defined in a separate PSP and you should carefully check RBAC controls to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to access that PSP.
input_row.audit: Get the set of PSPs with the following command: kubectl get psp For each PSP, check whether running containers as root is enabled: kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.runAsUser.rule}' Verify that there is at least one PSP which returns MustRunAsNonRoot or MustRunAs with the range of UIDs not including 0.
input_row.remediation: Create a PSP as described in the Kubernetes documentation, ensuring that the .spec.runAsUser.rule is set to either MustRunAsNonRoot or MustRunAs with the range of UIDs not including 0.
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which run as the root user will not be permitted.
input_row.default_value: By default, PodSecurityPolicies are not defined.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions kubectl commands to retrieve PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs) and their configurations, which can be executed without human judgment.
- The control can be verified through Kubernetes APIs and kubectl commands, specifically checking the `runAsUser.rule` field of the PSPs.
- The rationale emphasizes the need for a defined PSP that restricts root user access, which aligns with the ability to query this property via Kubernetes APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_root_containers_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get psp` to list all PodSecurityPolicies.
- For each PSP, use the command `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.runAsUser.rule}'` to check the `runAsUser.rule`.
- Validate that at least one PSP returns either `MustRunAsNonRoot` or `MustRunAs` with a range of UIDs that does not include 0.
- Pass criteria: At least one PSP must enforce non-root user execution; otherwise, it fails compliance.
